json_entry.title: When you feel utterly alone
json_entry.arabic: لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min adh-dhalimin
json_entry.translation: There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except You. Glory be to You! I have certainly done wrong.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite in moments of isolation, distress, or when you feel abandoned — as Prophet Yunus (AS) called out from the darkness of the whale's belly. Allah answered him; He will answer you.

ledger_row: | loneliness-1 | loneliness | Quran 21:87 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English translation verbatim from quran.com

notes: quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab), not Sahih International. The dua is the supplication portion of the verse only (not the full verse narrative). FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: During moments of distress and isolation
json_entry.arabic: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ الْعَظِيمُ الْحَلِيمُ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ رَبُّ السَّمَوَاتِ، وَرَبُّ الأَرْضِ، وَرَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْكَرِيمِ
json_entry.transliteration: La ilaha illallahu al-'Azim al-Halim, la ilaha illallahu Rabbul-'arsh il-'azim, la ilaha illallahu Rabbu-s-samawati wa Rabbul-ard wa Rabbul-'arsh il-karim
json_entry.translation: There is no god but Allah, the Magnificent, the Forbearing. There is no god but Allah, Lord of the Magnificent Throne. There is no god but Allah, Lord of the heavens, Lord of the earth, and Lord of the Noble Throne.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite at a time of distress (عِنْدَ الْكَرْبِ) — when loneliness, grief, or anxiety overwhelms you. The Prophet ﷺ used to say this during hardship.

ledger_row: | loneliness-2 | loneliness | Sahih al-Bukhari 6346 | sahih | Sunnah.com | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from sunnah.com

notes: Part of Sahih al-Bukhari — entire collection graded sahih. English translation as rendered on sunnah.com. Dua Arabic extracted from the full hadith text.

json_entry.title: Returning to Allah when you feel cut off
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ، أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ، أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَىَّ وَأَبُوءُ لَكَ بِذَنْبِي، فَاغْفِرْ لِي، فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma anta rabbi, la ilaha illa anta, khalaqtani wa ana 'abduka, wa ana 'ala 'ahdika wa wa'dika masta-ta'tu, a'udhu bika min sharri ma sana'tu, abu'u laka bini'matika 'alayya wa abu'u laka bidhanbi, faghfir li, fa innahu la yaghfiru-dh-dhunuba illa ant
json_entry.translation: O Allah, You are my Lord, there is none worthy of worship except You. You have created me, and I am Your servant, and I am faithful to Your covenant and promise as much as I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge Your blessings upon me, and I admit my sins. So forgive me, for none forgives sins except You.
json_entry.when_to_recite: The Master of Seeking Forgiveness (Sayyid al-Istighfar) — recite in the morning and evening, and whenever you feel spiritually distant or cut off from Allah. It is a declaration of belonging: 'You are my Lord.'

ledger_row: | loneliness-3 | loneliness | Sahih al-Bukhari 6306 | sahih | Sunnah.com | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from sunnah.com

notes: Sayyid al-Istighfar (Master of Seeking Forgiveness). Narrated by Shaddad bin Aus. Part of Sahih al-Bukhari — entire collection graded sahih.

json_entry.title: Surrendering to Allah — excerpt from the night prayer
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ رَبُّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ، لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ قَيِّمُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ، لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ نُورُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ، اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ أَسْلَمْتُ، وَبِكَ آمَنْتُ، وَعَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْتُ، فَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا قَدَّمْتُ وَمَا أَخَّرْتُ، وَأَسْرَرْتُ وَأَعْلَنْتُ، أَنْتَ إِلَهِي لاَ إِلَهَ لِي غَيْرُكَ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma lakal-hamdu anta Rabbu-s-samawati wal-ard, lakal-hamdu anta qayyimu-s-samawati wal-ardi wa man fihinn, lakal-hamdu anta nuru-s-samawati wal-ard, Allahumma laka aslamtu, wa bika amantu, wa 'alayka tawakkaltu, wa ilayka anabtu, faghfir li ma qaddamtu wa ma akhkhartu, wa asrartu wa a'lantu, anta ilahi la ilaha li ghairuk
json_entry.translation: O Allah: All the Praises are for You: You are the Lord of the Heavens and the Earth. All the Praises are for You; You are the Maintainer of the Heaven and the Earth and whatever is in them. All the Praises are for You; You are the Light of the Heavens and the Earth. O Allah! I surrender myself to You, and I believe in You and I depend upon You, and I repent to You. O Allah! Forgive me my sins that I did in the past or will do in the future, and also the sins I did in secret or in public. You are my only God (Whom I worship) and there is no other God for me.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When loneliness makes you feel the world has left you — declare to Allah: 'You are my Light, my Lord, my only companion.' This is an excerpt from a longer dua the Prophet ﷺ would say at night.

ledger_row: | loneliness-4 | loneliness | Sahih al-Bukhari 7385 | sahih | Sunnah.com | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English excerpt verified from sunnah.com

notes: Catalog title and usage now explicitly identify this as an excerpt from the longer night-prayer dua in Bukhari 7385.

json_entry.title: Relief from exhaustion, worry, and overwhelm
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَالْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ، وَضَلَعِ الدَّيْنِ، وَغَلَبَةِ الرِّجَالِ
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min al-hammi wal-hazani, wal-'ajzi wal-kasali, wal-jubni wal-bukhli, wa dhala'id-dayni, wa ghalabatir-rijal
json_entry.translation: O Allah! I seek refuge with You from worry and grief, from incapacity and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, from being heavily in debt and from being overpowered by (other) men.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When exhaustion, overwhelm, or a crushing sense of 'I can't go on' sets in. The Prophet ﷺ frequently recited this prayer — seeking Allah's protection from the very feelings that burnout brings: worry, grief, and incapacity.

ledger_row: | burnout-1 | burnout | Sahih al-Bukhari 6369 | sahih | Sunnah.com | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from sunnah.com

notes: Narrated by Anas bin Malik. Part of Sahih al-Bukhari. Covers hamm (worry), huzn (grief), 'ajz (incapacity), kasal (laziness) — directly relevant to burnout. Grade: sahih (Bukhari collection).

json_entry.title: Do not burden us beyond what we can bear
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَآ إِن نَّسِينَآ أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَآ إِصْرًۭا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِۦ ۖ وَٱعْفُ عَنَّا وَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَآ ۚ أَنتَ مَوْلَىٰنَا فَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana la tu'akhidhna in nasina aw akhta'na, Rabbana wa la tahmil 'alayna isran kama hamaltahu 'alal-ladhina min qablina, Rabbana wa la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bih, wa'fu 'anna waghfir lana warhamna, anta mawlana fansurna 'alal-qawmil-kafirin
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Do not punish us if we forget or make a mistake. Our Lord! Do not place a burden on us like the one you placed on those before us. Our Lord! Do not burden us with what we cannot bear. Pardon us, forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our ˹only˺ Guardian. So grant us victory over the disbelieving people.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When you feel crushed by life's weight and cannot carry on — Allah Himself promises He does not burden a soul beyond its capacity. This prayer is the closing supplication of Surah Al-Baqarah and beloved to recite morning and night.

ledger_row: | burnout-2 | burnout | Quran 2:286 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The dua begins at 'Rabbana la tu'akhidhna...' — the supplication portion of the verse. quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Musa's prayer when his people failed him
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ لَوْ شِئْتَ أَهْلَكْتَهُم مِّن قَبْلُ وَإِيَّـٰىَ ۖ أَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ مِنَّآ ۖ إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ تُضِلُّ بِهَا مَن تَشَآءُ وَتَهْدِى مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَا ۖ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْغَـٰفِرِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi law shi'ta ahlaktahum min qablu wa iyyaya, atuhlikuna bima fa'ala-s-sufaha'u minna, in hiya illa fitnatuka tudillu biha man tasha'u wa tahdi man tasha'u, anta waliyyuna faghfir lana warhamna wa anta khayrul-ghafirin
json_entry.translation: My Lord! Had You willed, You could have destroyed them long ago, and me as well. Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is only a test from You—by which You allow whoever You will to stray and guide whoever You will. You are our Guardian. So forgive us and have mercy on us. You are the best forgiver.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When you are burned out from carrying responsibility for others — leading, guiding, or caring while people around you fail. Musa (AS) prayed this when exhausted by his people's failures. Acknowledge that guidance is in Allah's hands, not yours alone.

ledger_row: | burnout-3 | burnout | Quran 7:155 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
notes: The catalog now includes the previously omitted clause, 'Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done?', preserving the full supplication segment from Musa (AS) within Quran 7:155.

json_entry.title: Grant us mercy and guide us through our ordeal
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةًۭ وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًۭا
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana atina min ladunka rahmatan wa hayyi' lana min amrina rashada
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Grant us mercy from Yourself and guide us rightly through our ordeal.
json_entry.when_to_recite: The prayer of the People of the Cave — young people who fled oppression and exhaustion, took refuge, and asked only for mercy and guidance. Recite when you have no energy left and need Allah to show you a way through.

ledger_row: | burnout-4 | burnout | Quran 18:10 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The dua is the supplication of the Ashab al-Kahf (People of the Cave). quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Asking Allah for a righteous and joyful marriage
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٖ وَٱجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana hab lana min azwajina wa dhurriyyatina qurrata a'yunin waj'alna lil-muttaqina imama
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Bless us with ˹pious˺ spouses and offspring who will be the joy of our hearts, and make us models for the righteous.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When you are experiencing conflict in your marriage — ask Allah not for the conflict to disappear, but for Him to make your spouse and family the joy of your eyes. This prayer shifts perspective from grievance to aspiration.

ledger_row: | marriage_conflict-1 | marriage_conflict | Quran 25:74 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The dua of the 'Ibad al-Rahman (servants of the Most Merciful). quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Grant us goodness in this life and the next
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةًۭ وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ حَسَنَةًۭ وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Grant us the good of this world and the Hereafter, and protect us from the torment of the Fire.
json_entry.when_to_recite: The most comprehensive prayer in the Quran — recite during marital difficulties to ask Allah for what is truly good, including goodness in your marriage and home, while keeping your gaze on what endures beyond this world.

ledger_row: | marriage_conflict-2 | marriage_conflict | Quran 2:201 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The most frequently recited Quranic dua, described in Bukhari/Muslim as the best dua. quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Join our hearts and mend what is between us
json_entry.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَلِّفْ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِنَا وَأَصْلِحْ ذَاتَ بَيْنِنَا وَاهْدِنَا سُبُلَ السَّلَامِ وَنَجِّنَا مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَجَنِّبْنَا الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي أَسْمَاعِنَا وَأَبْصَارِنَا وَقُلُوبِنَا وَأَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ وَاجْعَلْنَا شَاكِرِينَ لِنِعْمَتِكَ مُثْنِينَ بِهَا قَابِلِيهَا وَأَتِمَّهَا عَلَيْنَا
json_entry.transliteration: Allahumma allif bayna qulubina wa aslih dhata baynina, wahdina subula-s-salam, wa najjina mina-dh-dhulumati ila-n-nur, wa jannibna-l-fawahisha ma zahara minha wa ma batan, wa barik lana fi asma'ina wa absarina wa qulubina wa azwajina wa dhurriyyatina, wa tub 'alayna innaka anta-t-Tawwabu-r-Rahim, waj'alna shakirin li-ni'matika muthnina biha qabiliha wa atimmaha 'alayna
json_entry.translation: O Allah, join our hearts, mend what is between us, guide us to the paths of peace, bring us from darkness to light, keep us away from obscenities, outward and inward, and bless our hearing, our sight, our hearts, our spouses, and our children. Turn toward us, for You are the Accepter of repentance, the Most Merciful. Make us grateful for Your blessing, praising it, accepting it, and complete it upon us.
json_entry.when_to_recite: During marital tension, family strain, or any conflict where hearts need reconciliation and Allah's guidance toward peace.

ledger_row: | marriage_conflict-3 | marriage_conflict | Sunan Abi Dawud 969 | sahih | Sunnah.com (Al-Albani grading) | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic text and English meaning verified from sunnah.com
Grade: Sahih per Al-Albani, shown on sunnah.com page

notes: Replaced Abu Dawud 2160 because its original context is a husband's dua when entering marriage. Abu Dawud 969 is a stronger fit for conflict: 'join our hearts' and 'mend what is between us,' graded Sahih by Al-Albani on sunnah.com.

json_entry.title: Do not let our hearts deviate from one another
json_entry.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْوَهَّابُ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbana la tuzigh qulubana ba'da idh hadaytana wa hab lana min ladunka rahmah, innaka anta-l-Wahhab
json_entry.translation: Our Lord! Do not let our hearts deviate after you have guided us. Grant us Your mercy. You are indeed the Giver ˹of all bounties˺.
json_entry.when_to_recite: When conflict in your marriage makes hearts feel hardened or distant from each other — ask Allah, the Turner of Hearts, to keep your hearts aligned with His guidance and softened toward your spouse.

ledger_row: | marriage_conflict-4 | marriage_conflict | Quran 3:8 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The dua of the Rasikhun fil-'Ilm (those firm in knowledge). Allah is Al-Wahhab (The Giver of Gifts). quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Ibrahim's prayer for praying children
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْنِى مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَآءِ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbij-'alni muqimas-salati wa min dhurriyyati, Rabbana wa taqabbal du'a'
json_entry.translation: My Lord! Make me and those ˹believers˺ of my descendants keep up prayer. Our Lord! Accept my prayers.
json_entry.when_to_recite: The prayer of Prophet Ibrahim (AS) — a father who worried about his children's spiritual future. Recite for your children: ask Allah to establish prayer in their hearts and accept your duas for them.

ledger_row: | parenting-1 | parenting | Quran 14:40 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: Ibrahim's dua for his children establishing salah. quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Zakariyya's prayer for righteous children
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ هَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةًۭ طَيِّبَةً ۖ إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi hab li min ladunka dhurriyyatan tayyibah, innaka sami'ud-du'a'
json_entry.translation: My Lord! Grant me—by your grace—righteous offspring. You are certainly the Hearer of ˹all˺ prayers.
json_entry.when_to_recite: The prayer of Prophet Zakariyya (AS) — said when he longed for a child who would be righteous and carry on goodness. Recite when hoping for children, or when praying for a child who has strayed, trusting that Allah hears every prayer.

ledger_row: | parenting-2 | parenting | Quran 3:38 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: Zakariyya's dua for a righteous child — answered with Yahya (John the Baptist). quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: A parent's prayer for righteous children
json_entry.arabic: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَـٰلِحًۭا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِى فِى ذُرِّيَّتِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّى مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
json_entry.transliteration: Rabbi awzi'ni an ashkura ni'matakallati an'amta 'alayya wa 'ala walidayya wa an a'mala salihan tardahu wa aslih li fi dhurriyyati, inni tubtu ilayka wa inni minal-muslimin
json_entry.translation: My Lord! Inspire me to ˹always˺ be thankful for Your favours which You blessed me and my parents with, and to do good deeds that please You. And instil righteousness in my offspring. I truly repent to You, and I truly submit ˹to Your Will˺.
json_entry.when_to_recite: Recite at forty — or any time you feel the weight of parenthood. Ask Allah to make you a grateful child and a righteous parent, and to plant goodness in your children's hearts. 'Aslih li fi dhurriyyati' — rectify my offspring for me.

ledger_row: | parenting-3 | parenting | Quran 46:15 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |

provenance.claim: Arabic and English verbatim from quran.com

notes: The dua of the mature believer at forty — explicitly includes asking for rectification of offspring (wa aslih li fi dhurriyyati). quran.com default translation is The Clear Quran. FLAG for scholar review of translation choice.

json_entry.title: Grant me an heir who will be pleasing to You
json_entry.arabic: فَهَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّۭا ۝ يَرِثُنِى وَيَرِثُ مِنْ ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَٱجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّۭا
json_entry.transliteration: Fa hab li min ladunka waliyya, yarithuni wa yarithu min ali ya'quba, waj'alhu Rabbi radiyya
json_entry.translation: So grant me, by Your grace, an heir who will inherit ˹prophethood˺ from me and the family of Jacob, and make him, O Lord, pleasing ˹to You˺!
json_entry.when_to_recite: Zakariyya (AS) was old and his wife barren, yet he asked Allah for a child who would be righteous and pleasing to Him — not for his own satisfaction, but for the continuation of goodness. Recite when longing for children or praying that your children grow up beloved to Allah.

ledger_row: | parenting-4 | parenting | Quran 19:5-6 | Quran | The Clear Quran (Dr. Mustafa Khattab) — quran.com default | Claude-assisted, pending scholar review | 2026-05-13 |
